steps.title: Read Lady Howard’s concern
steps.content: Lady Howard urges Reverend Villars to let Evelina join the Mirvans’ trip because it is time for Evelina to “see something of the world.” She warns that young people who are too rigidly sheltered imagine the world unrealistically, while proper exposure helps them see both pleasure and disappointment.

steps.title: Eliminate claims beyond the text
steps.content: Choice A focuses on shielding Evelina from other young people, but Lady Howard focuses on shielding her from the world generally. Choice B says Villars gave Evelina his own idealistic view, which the text does not say. Choice C invents a mistrust of the Mirvans. Lady Howard’s point is that Villars has protected Evelina too much and that more experience would help her. The answer is D.
